title: Advanced SQL
learningObjectives: Explain **Advanced SQL** with production trade-offs, SLO thinking, and failure modes
Implement small **Java** snippets that anchor interview whiteboard discussions
Connect **observability** and **capacity** signals to real operational decisions
Identify common **pitfalls** that cause incidents or cost surprises at scale
Compare alternatives using **constraints** (latency, cost, team skills, compliance)
Present answers with **structure**: problem, options, decision, verification

sections.title: Why Advanced SQL matters
sections.content: **SQL** is still how **money** and **truth** meet in most systems. Senior interviews reward **EXPLAIN** discipline, **index** reasoning, and honest talk about **isolation** trade-offs—not memorized joins alone.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Advanced SQL
sections.content:
### Day 68 — **Advanced** **SQL**

### 1. Access paths
**Seek**, **scan**, **bitmap**, **index-only** plans.

### 2. Join algorithms
**Nested** loops vs **hash** vs **merge**—cardinality matters.

### 3. Windows
**Ranking** and **running** aggregates without losing row detail.

### 4. Transactions
**Isolation**, **locks**, **deadlocks**, **retries**.

### 5. ORM reality
**N+1**, **batching**, **fetch** joins.

### 6. Story
**10M** **rows** pending orders—composite **index** on **(status, created_at)** plus **covering** includes for hot columns.

sections.title: Basic — JOIN printed as discussion anchor
sections.output: SELECT u.name, o.id FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id

sections.title: Intermediate — Selectivity toy
Advanced — Window clause string
sections.output: ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY user_id ORDER BY created_at DESC)

sections.title: Query planner path
sections.description: Predicate pushes into index seek when selective.

sections.title: Exercise — Advanced SQL (Day 68 assignment)
sections.problem:
Print **exactly** two lines:
1. Implemented: Advanced SQL
2. Focus: Tune pending orders query on 10M rows.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is a **covering** **index** and when does it win?
conceptual.answer: Index includes all selected columns so the engine avoids heap lookups for that query pattern. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work. Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Advanced SQL**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Index includes all selected columns so the engine avoids heap lookups for that query pattern. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. Index includes all selected columns so the engine avoids heap lookups for that query patte Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: Explain **SARGable** predicates.
conceptual.answer: Predicates that allow index use—avoid wrapping indexed columns in functions. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast.

conceptual.question: **Window** **functions** vs **GROUP** **BY** for “latest row per key”?
conceptual.answer: Windows keep detail rows; group-by collapses—pick based on whether you need ties or ranking. Mention **verification**: **`curl -v`** through **gateway**, **`kubectl logs`**, **APM** **waterfall** **spans**, and **dashboards** that slice **p99** by **downstream** **service** **name**. Senior answers separate **symptom** from **root** **cause**: **502** at **edge** can be **empty** **Endpoints**, **stale** **pool**, or **upstream** **panic**—each needs different **first** **commands**.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Recall |
|---|---|
| Covering index | Avoid heap fetch |
| SARGable | Predicate shape |
| Window | Rank without collapse |
| Isolation | Anomalies vs locks |
| N+1 | ORM batching |
| Deadlock | Lock order |
| EXPLAIN | Estimates vs actual |
| Seek vs scan | Selectivity |
| Composite index | Leading column rules |
| Vacuum/analyze | Planner stats |
